name: Gateron KS-33 Magnetic Hall-Effect
summary: Full-size analog hall-effect switch with adjustable actuation — the most widely available magnetic switch for DIY builds
pinDescription: VCC, GND, Analog Signal (variable voltage proportional to stem position)
hotswapNote: Requires Gateron magnetic 3-pin hot-swap sockets — NOT compatible with standard MX Kailh/Gateron mechanical sockets
scanningNote: Requires per-key ADC reading via analog multiplexers (e.g., CD74HC4067). NOT compatible with standard diode matrix scanning. Typical setup: 16:1 analog mux → MCU ADC channel.
footprintNote: No official KiCad footprint published. Pin positions must be measured from physical samples. 3-pin through-hole layout differs from standard 2-pin MX switches.
designNotes: Hall-effect switches output analog voltage — NO diode matrix scanning. Requires ADC + multiplexer architecture.
Each switch needs 3 connections: VCC (3.3V), GND, and analog signal trace routed to a multiplexer input.
Typical architecture: 16:1 analog multiplexers (CD74HC4067) → MCU ADC pins. An 86-key board needs ~6 mux ICs.
The nRF52840 has 8 analog inputs (SAADC) — sufficient to read 6 multiplexers with 2 spare channels.
Adjustable actuation point is a firmware feature — the MCU reads analog values and applies software thresholds.
ZMK does not natively support analog scanning — requires custom driver development.
Pin dimensions not publicly documented — physical measurement required for accurate PCB footprint.
features: True analog output — enables rapid trigger, adjustable sensitivity per key
Contactless operation — no metal contacts, theoretically infinite lifespan
No key bounce/debounce needed — instant digital readout from analog threshold
Standard MX cross (+) stem — compatible with all MX keycaps
Most widely available hall-effect switch for individual purchase
concerns: Requires completely different PCB scanning architecture (ADC + multiplexers, not standard matrix)
ZMK firmware does not natively support analog scanning — custom drivers needed
No official KiCad footprint — pin positions must be reverse-engineered from samples
Hot-swap sockets are Gateron magnetic 3-pin (NOT standard MX hot-swap)
Slightly more expensive than mechanical switches ($0.40–0.70/switch vs $0.15–0.30)
Full MX height only — no low-profile version of the KS-33
